report_name: Global Consulting Services Breach Exposes PII of 1,320 Individuals

attack_patterns.name: Data from Local System
attack_patterns.description: An unauthorized third party accessed and collected sensitive personally identifiable information, including names and Social Security numbers, from the company's network.

attack_patterns.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
attack_patterns.description: An unauthorized third party exfiltrated a data set containing sensitive personally identifiable information from the company's network.

campaigns.name: January 2026 Global Consulting Services Breach
campaigns.description: A data breach incident in January 2026 where an unauthorized third party accessed and exfiltrated the personally identifiable information of 1,320 individuals from the network of Global Consulting Services & Software Development.

identities.name: Global Consulting Services & Software Development
identities.description: A California-based IT consulting and software development firm that suffered a data breach exposing the names and Social Security numbers of 1,320 individuals.

identities.name: Kroll
identities.description: The company providing 24 months of free identity monitoring and restoration services to individuals affected by the Global Consulting Services data breach.

indicators.name: Data Staging via Archive File Creation
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential Data Staging via Archive File Creation
id: 7b9c1d2e-f3a4-4b5c-8d9e-0f1a2b3c4d5e
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the creation of large, compressed archive files (e.g., .zip, .rar, .7z) on servers, which could indicate an attacker is staging data for exfiltration. This was suggested as a hunting hint in the report on the Global Consulting Services breach.
references:
  - https://www.jdsupra.com/legalnews/global-consulting-services-data-breach-1100418/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
modified: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1560.001
  - attack.collection
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.zip'
      - '.rar'
      - '.7z'
      - '.tar.gz'
      - '.tgz'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate administrative or user activity involving file archiving.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the creation of archive files, a common technique for staging data before exfiltration, as suggested by the hunting hints in the report.

indicators.name: Suspicious Find Command for Sensitive Files
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Find Command for Sensitive Files
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the use of the 'find' command to search for potentially sensitive files, such as those with a .csv extension, across the entire filesystem. This was provided as a hunting hint for detecting breaches similar to the one at Global Consulting Services.
references:
  - https://www.jdsupra.com/legalnews/global-consulting-services-data-breach-1100418/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-20
modified: 2026-05-20
tags:
  - attack.t1083
  - attack.discovery
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: linux
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '/find'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - ' / '
      - '-name'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - '*.csv'
      - '*.sql'
      - '*.bak'
      - '*dump*'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate system administration scripts or user searches.
level: medium
indicators.description: Detects the use of the `find` command to locate potentially sensitive files, based on hunting hints provided in the article.

relationships.source_name: Data Staging via Archive File Creation
Suspicious Find Command for Sensitive Files
